pubmed_links: [Sirmaci et al. (2011) Mutations in ANKRD11 cause KBG syndrome, characterized by intellectual disability, skeletal malformations, and macrodontia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21782149/); [Lo-Castro et al. (2013) Neurobehavioral phenotype observed in KBG syndrome caused by ANKRD11 mutations](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23184435/); [Ka and Kim (2018) ANKRD11 associated with intellectual disability and autism regulates dendrite differentiation via the BDNF/TrkB signaling pathway](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29274743/)
summary: Haploinsufficiency of ANKRD11 is the established cause of KBG syndrome, a condition characterized by intellectual disability, autism spectrum disorder, ADHD, OCD, and other neurobehavioral features; de novo loss-of-function variants in ANKRD11 have also been independently identified in large ASD sequencing studies reaching exome-wide significance. (well-established)
